summary: The scanner is still a monolith with shared mutable globals, mixed discovery/execution concerns, and multiple fallback paths in one file. The refactor should isolate pure utilities first, then move discovery, file execution, runtime bookkeeping, and finally orchestration.

affected_surfaces.impact: Primary orchestration and legacy export surface
Current home of pattern definitions, dedupe, comment helpers, discovery, file execution, and progress/cache logic

blast_radius: Public exports from scanner.ts must remain stable
Custom pattern registration still needs to work through ScannerRegistry
Incremental cache updates must not regress unchanged-file hit behavior
Tree-sitter and AST paths must continue to fall back to regex in the same cases
Parallel and sequential execution must emit the same elements and cache updates
risks: Hidden behavior drift from moving shared globals into per-call resolved state
Deduplication order changes if pattern priority is not preserved exactly
File discovery drift if path normalization or tsx/jsx remapping changes
Progress callback drift if cache hits, failures, and parallel mode do not report identically
ScannerRegistry compatibility drift if callers relied on mutation of shared defaults
test_strategy: Add characterization tests before any extraction work
Lock recursive versus non-recursive discovery semantics
Lock exclude matching and custom pattern behavior
Lock incremental cache, AST fallback, tree-sitter fallback, and progress behavior
Compare sequential and parallel runs against the same fixture set
